title: Smoky Syrian Lamb Kebabs
description: Fermented, tender lamb kebabs infused with bay leaves and smoky spices, perfect for a lowcarb lazy Sunday grill.
instructions: In a bowl whisk together yogurt, lemon juice, minced garlic, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and salt until smooth.
Add lamb cubes to the bowl, ensuring each piece is coated; cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours or overnight for maximum tenderness.
Preheat a charcoal or gas grill to medium‑high heat (around 400°F/200°C).
Thread lamb cubes onto skewers, inserting a bay leaf between each piece for flavor and easy handling.
Brush the kebabs with olive oil and grill 3–4 minutes per side, or until a char forms and internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C) for medium‑rare or 160°F (71°C) for fully cooked.
Remove from grill and let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes to retain juices.
Sprinkle chopped fresh mint over the kebabs and serve immediately, optionally with a side of grilled vegetables or a cucumber‑yogurt sauce.
